reasoning: The base rate for developing and approving a new vaccine is on the order of many years to decades. There is currently no approved vaccine (human or animal) for Cat-scratch disease (Bartonella henselae), and web searches show no evidence of any candidate in advanced (Phase 3) trials. It is highly improbable that a vaccine will be created, verified, and listed on the Wikipedia page "List of infectious diseases" within the next 6 months. Any non-zero probability is primarily due to the small risk of un-reverted vandalism on the resolution dates or unexpected definitional changes, which slightly increases with longer horizons.
